{{Quote|Open fire on the shields! ''Kusurrun'', you will dive onto the shield over the enemy command center at maximum thrust!|Warmaster Tsavong Lah, during the Battle of Ebaq|<ref name="Way" />}}
'''''Kusurrun''''' was a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[ro'ik chuun m'arh]] [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] that took part in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] that was, for the most part, controlled by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Part of the [[Battle Group of Yun-Yuuzhan]], ''Kusurrun'' participated in the [[Battle of Ebaq]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], in which Yuuzhan Vong forces [[Space warfare/Legends|engaged]] those of the New Republic. During the conflict, [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] had ''Kusurrun'' immolate itself upon the [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]] covering the [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] [[Ebaq 9]] in order to bring them down.

==Description==
''Kusurrun'' was a [[ro'ik chuun m'arh]]<ref name="NJOS" /> [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] used by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Way" />

==History==
In existence by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]],<ref name="Way" /><ref name="ERC" /> during an [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] controlled mainly by the government of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], ''Kusurrun'' participated in the the [[Battle of Ebaq]]. There, ''Kusurrun'' was a part of the [[Battle Group of Yun-Yuuzhan]], which was commanded by [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]], as Lah attempted to destroy a New Republic [[Ebaq Control|base]] on the [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] of [[Ebaq 9]] in the [[Treskov system]]; the base was actually bait for a trap that the New Republic's [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] had designed to give his side a much-needed victory against the [[Yuuzhan Vong empire]]. As the [[Space warfare/Legends|battle]] began to look as if the New Republic would defeat the Yuuzhan Vong forces in the Treskov [[Star system/Legends|system]], Lah had ''Kusurrun'' and the rest of the Battle Group of Yun-Yuuzhan, along with some of his other forces, head for Ebaq 9, where a couple of New Republic–allied [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] were stationed, so that the Warmaster might [[Death/Legends|kill]] them.<ref name="Way" />

Hoping to bring down the [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]] over Ebaq 9, Lah ordered ''Kusurrun'' to commit a suicide run on the shields to damage them, and also had the [[Battle group/Legends|battle group's]] [[Victory Section]] follow the frigate to take out the shield with their [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulating [[dovin basal]]s. The frigate did as ordered, impacting the shield over the enemy command center on Ebaq 9 at top speed and destroying itself, though the shields held despite Victory Section's best efforts. However, the Warmaster eventually managed to get the shields down, after ordering at least one more frigate to make a [[Ramming/Legends|suicide run]], and landed his [[Warrior caste|warriors]] on the planetoid. Despite this, the battle ended in a spectacular victory for the New Republic, with Lah perishing in the fighting.<ref name="Way" />

==Behind the scenes==
''Kusurrun'' appeared in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'', the fourteenth novel in the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' series. It was published in [[2002]] and written by [[Walter Jon Williams]].<ref name="Way" />

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' {{1st}}
